perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement the term "Proprietary Information" shall mean any information, whether in writing or other documentary form, in oral or visual or machine readable form, or in the form of algorithms, samples or models, disclosed by one Party (the "Disclosing Party" ) to another Party (the "Receiving Party") under this Agreement, provided that: (a) where such information is in writing or other documentary form, it is clearly and conspicuously marked at the time of disclosure as proprietary or commercially sensitive (for example with a marking such as "Proprietary" or "Commercial -in- Confidence").; or V1.2 COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENCE (WHEN COMPLETE) 2 COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENC E (WHEN COMPLETE) (b) where such information is disclosed in oral or visual or machine readable form, or in the form of samples or models, it is designated proprietary or commercially sensitive at the time of disclosure and is confirmed by the Disclosing Party as such in documentary form within thirty (30) days from its being disclosed, in which event all the protections and restricti ons in this Agreement as to the use and disclosure of said Proprietary information shall apply retrospectively during the said period of thirty days. Proprietary Information shall also include any information which can be obtained by examination, testing or analysis of any hardware, software or material samples provided by the Disclosing Party, notwithstanding that the requirements for marking and designation referred to above shall not have been fulfilled.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement the term "Proprietary Information" shall mean any information, whether in writing or other documentary form, in oral or visual or machine readable form, or in the form of algorithms, samples or models, disclosed by one Party (the "Disclosing Party" ) to another Party (the "Receiving Party") under this Agreement, provided that: (a) where such information is in writing or other documentary form, it is clearly and conspicuously marked at the time of disclosure as proprietary or commercially sensitive (for example with a marking such as "Proprietary" or "Commercial -in- Confidence").; or V1.2 COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENCE (WHEN COMPLETE) 2 COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENC E (WHEN COMPLETE) (b) where such information is disclosed in oral or visual or machine readable form, or in the form of samples or models, it is designated proprietary or commercially sensitive at the time of disclosure and is confirmed by the Disclosing Party as such in documentary form within thirty (30) days from its being disclosed, in which event all the protections and restricti ons in this Agreement as to the use and disclosure of said Proprietary information shall apply retrospectively during the said period of thirty days. Proprietary Information shall not include any information which can be obtained by examination, testing or analysis of any hardware, software or material samples provided by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that Proprietary Information shall *not* include information obtained through examination, testing, or analysis of hardware/software, I'm directly contradicting the original statement in Clause 1 that it *shall* include such information. This creates an in-text contradiction within Clause 1 regarding the scope of "Proprietary Information". The contradiction affects the subsequent clauses, especially Clause 2, which outlines obligations related to Proprietary Information. If information gained from analysis isn't considered Proprietary Information due to this change, those obligations might not apply, creating enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement the term "Proprietary Information" shall mean any information, whether in writing or other documentary form, in oral or visual or machine readable form, or in the form of algorithms, samples or models, disclosed by one Party (the "Disclosing Party" ) to another Party (the "Receiving Party") under this Agreement, provided that: (a) where such information is in writing or other documentary form, it is clearly and conspicuously marked at the time of disclosure as proprietary or commercially sensitive (for example with a marking such as "Proprietary" or "Commercial -in- Confidence").; or V1.2 COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENCE (WHEN COMPLETE) 2 COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENC E (WHEN COMPLETE) (b) where such information is disclosed in oral or visual or machine readable form, or in the form of samples or models, it is designated proprietary or commercially sensitive at the time of disclosure and is confirmed by the Disclosing Party as such in documentary form within thirty (30) days from its being disclosed, in which event all the protections and restricti ons in this Agreement as to the use and disclosure of said Proprietary information shall apply retrospectively during the said period of thirty days. Proprietary Information shall not include any information which can be obtained by examination, testing or analysis of any hardware, software or material samples provided by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original definition of 'Proprietary Information' within the same clause. The original text explicitly states that Proprietary Information 'shall also include any information which can be obtained by examination, testing or analysis...', while the changed text states that Proprietary Information 'shall not include any information which can be obtained by examination, testing or analysis...'. This is a clear and direct conflict that creates an in-text contradiction, affecting the enforceability of subsequent clauses related to Proprietary Information.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions of Clauses 4 and 7 hereof, the Receiving Party undertakes: (a) to keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, except with the written permission of the Disclosing Party, any part, or the whole, of any Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreem ent; (b) not to copy Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement except as is reasonably necessary for the Purpose; (c) not to use Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement other than for the Purpose, except with th e prior written permission of the Disclosing Party; (d) to restrict access to the Proprietary Information disclosed to it under the terms of this Agreement to those of its employees and officers who need to know the same for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the provisions of Clauses 4 and 7 hereof, the Receiving Party undertakes: (a) to keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, except with the written permission of the Disclosing Party, any part, or the whole, of any Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreem ent; (b) not to copy Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement except as is reasonably necessary for the Purpose, unless copying is explicitly prohibited by the disclosing party in writing; (c) not to use Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement other than for the Purpose, except with th e prior written permission of the Disclosing Party, or unless such use is required by law; (d) to restrict access to the Proprietary Information disclosed to it under the terms of this Agreement to only senior management, regardless of their need to know for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless copying is explicitly prohibited by the disclosing party in writing" to Clause 2(b), I'm creating a loophole. The original clause implied a general restriction on copying, but the new clause suggests copying is allowed unless specifically forbidden. This contradicts the general confidentiality obligation initially established, creating enforcement uncertainty. Additionally, changing Clause 2(d) to restrict access 'to only senior management' directly contradicts the original clause that states access should be limited to those who 'need to know the same for the Purpose'. This new restriction creates uncertainty about who has access to proprietary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions of Clauses 4 and 7 hereof, the Receiving Party undertakes: (a) to keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, except with the written permission of the Disclosing Party, any part, or the whole, of any Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreem ent; (b) not to copy Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement except as is reasonably necessary for the Purpose, unless copying is explicitly prohibited by the disclosing party in writing; (c) not to use Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement other than for the Purpose, except with th e prior written permission of the Disclosing Party, or unless such use is required by law; (d) to restrict access to the Proprietary Information disclosed to it under the terms of this Agreement to only senior management, regardless of their need to know for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces two potential contradictions. First, by adding "unless copying is explicitly prohibited by the disclosing party in writing" to Clause 2(b), it creates a loophole allowing copying unless forbidden, which weakens the original confidentiality obligation. Second, modifying Clause 2(d) to restrict access to 'only senior management' regardless of need directly contradicts the original intent of limiting access based on 'need to know for the Purpose.' Since the Changed Text and Contradicted text are in the same location, we can clearly see that the Change creates a contradiction, based on the explanation. The Changed version directly limits access to only Senior Management which contradicts with the original version which allows more employees to have access as long as its for the Purpose. This adheres to the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The protec tions and restrictions in this Agreement as to the use and disclosure of Proprietary Information shall not apply to any information which the Receiving Party can show by written and dated or datable material: (a) is, at the time of disclosure hereunder, already published or otherwise publicly available; or (b) is, after disclosure hereunder, published or becomes available to the public other than by breach of this Agreement; or (c) is rightfully in the Receiving Party's possession with rights to us e and/or disclose, prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (d) is rightfully disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party with rights to use and/or disclose; or (e) is independently developed by or for the Receiving Party without referenc e or access to Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder; or (f) is the subject of a requirement of a court proceeding requirement on the Receiving Party for disclosure, in which event the Receiving Party shall give prompt notice thereof to the Disclosi ng Party to the extent permissible by law..
perturbation.changed_text: The protec tions and restrictions in this Agreement as to the use and disclosure of Proprietary Information shall not apply to any information which the Receiving Party can show by written and dated or datable material: (a) is, at the time of disclosure hereunder, already published or otherwise publicly available, even if the receiving party was responsible for publishing it; or (b) is, after disclosure hereunder, published or becomes available to the public other than by breach of this Agreement; or (c) is rightfully in the Receiving Party's possession with rights to us e and/or disclose, prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (d) is rightfully disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party with rights to use and/or disclose; or (e) is independently developed by or for the Receiving Party without referenc e or access to Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder; or (f) is the subject of a requirement of a court proceeding requirement on the Receiving Party for disclosure, in which event the Receiving Party shall give prompt notice thereof to the Disclosi ng Party to the extent permissible by law..
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'even if the receiving party was responsible for publishing it' to clause 3(a), I'm creating a contradiction. The original clause implies that information already publicly available is exempt from protection. The new clause makes it exempt even if the *Receiving Party* caused it to be publicly available. This directly contradicts the intent of the NDA, which is to prevent unauthorized disclosure. This creates enforcement uncertainty. It directly contradicts the fundamental obligation of confidentiality outlined in Clause 2. If the receiving party can disclose the information and then claim it's publicly available, Clause 2 becomes meaningless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions of Clauses 4 and 7 hereof, the Receiving Party undertakes: (a) to keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, except with the written permission of the Disclosing Party, any part, or the whole, of any Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreem ent; (b) not to copy Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement except as is reasonably necessary for the Purpose, unless copying is explicitly prohibited by the disclosing party in writing; (c) not to use Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement other than for the Purpose, except with th e prior written permission of the Disclosing Party, or unless such use is required by law; (d) to restrict access to the Proprietary Information disclosed to it under the terms of this Agreement to only senior management, regardless of their need to know for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "even if the receiving party was responsible for publishing it" to the exceptions regarding proprietary information. This directly contradicts clause 2(a) in the 'Contradicted Text', which requires the Receiving Party to keep Proprietary Information confidential and not disclose it. The change allows the Receiving Party to disclose the information and then claim it is publicly available, which makes the confidentiality obligation outlined in clause 2(a) unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation also details this contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remai n in force for a period of two (2) years following the date first above written after which it will terminate unless renewed by mutual consent in writing or to the extent that it is superseded by another agreement or contract between the Parties. The obli gations and restrictions relating to the disclosure and use of Proprietary Information shall however survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of seven (7) years.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remai n in force for a period of two (2) years following the date first above written after which it will terminate unless renewed by mutual consent in writing or to the extent that it is superseded by another agreement or contract between the Parties. The obli gations and restrictions relating to the disclosure and use of Proprietary Information shall however survive the termination of this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that confidentiality obligations survive for seven years post-termination. The changed text states that the obligations survive 'indefinitely'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligation, creating uncertainty about its enforceability. This contradicts the time limitation previously stated, leading to uncertainty regarding the duration of the obligations after the agreement terminates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remai n in force for a period of two (2) years following the date first above written after which it will terminate unless renewed by mutual consent in writing or to the extent that it is superseded by another agreement or contract between the Parties. The obli gations and restrictions relating to the disclosure and use of Proprietary Information shall however survive the termination of this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that confidentiality obligations survive for seven years post-termination, while the changed text states that the obligations survive 'indefinitely'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligation, creating uncertainty about its enforceability. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but that doesn't mean there isn't a contradiction, as it's compared to the original text. According to the Perturbation explanation, this creates a direct contradiction because it contradicts the time limitation previously stated, leading to uncertainty regarding the duration of the obligations after the agreement terminates. Therefore, this is a structural flaw that causes an in-text contradiction as per the definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire existing Agreement between the Parti es concerning the exchange of Proprietary Information for the Purpose. The Agreement shall not be amended except by written agreement signed by authorised representatives of all Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire existing Agreement between the Parti es concerning the exchange of Proprietary Information for the Purpose. The Agreement can be amended by either party at any time with electronic notification.
perturbation.explanation: The original Clause 8 explicitly states that amendments require a written agreement signed by all authorized representatives. The modified clause states the Agreement can be amended by either party at any time with electronic notification. This creates a direct contradiction on how amendments can be made. This creates considerable uncertainty and would be unenforceable. It contradicts the entire intention of having a signed agreement, which is supposed to be a fixed set of rules.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire existing Agreement between the Parti es concerning the exchange of Proprietary Information for the Purpose. The Agreement can be amended by either party at any time with electronic notification.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments require a written agreement signed by authorized representatives of all parties. The changed text states that the agreement can be amended by either party at any time with electronic notification. This creates a direct contradiction in the method of amending the agreement as the contradicted text (which is a copy of the changed text) allows for unilateral amendments, where the original stated amendments require authorized representatives. The contradiction is whether amendments require agreement of all parties or not, with the changed version enabling amendments by either party.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall attempt to solve any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement by means of alternative dispute resolution such as b ut without limitation mediati on or mini -trial. Upon the occurrence of the dispute, the Parties shall define the type and the rules for the implementation of such alternative resolution. The Parties expressly agree that after a period of two (2) months after the occurrence of the dis pute and provided that the dispute remains unsolved, it shall be finally settled under the rules of Arbitration of the London Court of International Arbitration by one or more arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said rules. The place of arbitratio n shall be London (UK). The language of arbitration shall be the English Language. The Parties hereby agree to exclude the right of appeal to the High Court under section 69 of the Arbitration Act 1996 and the right for an application to be made to the Hi gh Court under the corresponding section of the Act. Each Party recognises and acknowledges that in the event of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement it shall have the right to apply for injunctive relief or other appropriate orders before an y competent court to restrain the other as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall attempt to solve any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement by means of alternative dispute resolution such as b ut without limitation mediati on or mini -trial. Upon the occurrence of the dispute, each party is free to pursue litigation. The Parties expressly agree that after a period of two (2) months after the occurrence of the dis pute and provided that the dispute remains unsolved, it shall be finally settled under the rules of Arbitration of the London Court of International Arbitration by one or more arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said rules. The place of arbitratio n shall be London (UK). The language of arbitration shall be the English Language. The Parties hereby agree to exclude the right of appeal to the High Court under section 69 of the Arbitration Act 1996 and the right for an application to be made to the Hi gh Court under the corresponding section of the Act. Each Party recognises and acknowledges that in the event of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement it shall have the right to apply for injunctive relief or other appropriate orders before an y competent court to restrain the other as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: By stating 'Upon the occurrence of the dispute, each party is free to pursue litigation', I create a contradiction with the rest of Clause 12, which specifies a mandatory arbitration process. The statement creates uncertainty about whether disputes should be resolved through litigation or arbitration, undermining the entire dispute resolution mechanism. This statement nullifies the binding arbitration agreement and creates the possiblity of litigation, while later in the text of clause 12, arbitration is described, thus creating conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall attempt to solve any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement by means of alternative dispute resolution such as b ut without limitation mediati on or mini -trial. Upon the occurrence of the dispute, each party is free to pursue litigation. The Parties expressly agree that after a period of two (2) months after the occurrence of the dis pute and provided that the dispute remains unsolved, it shall be finally settled under the rules of Arbitration of the London Court of International Arbitration by one or more arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said rules. The place of arbitratio n shall be London (UK). The language of arbitration shall be the English Language. The Parties hereby agree to exclude the right of appeal to the High Court under section 69 of the Arbitration Act 1996 and the right for an application to be made to the Hi gh Court under the corresponding section of the Act. Each Party recognises and acknowledges that in the event of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement it shall have the right to apply for injunctive relief or other appropriate orders before an y competent court to restrain the other as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text commits the parties to alternative dispute resolution followed by arbitration. The changed text allows 'each party is free to pursue litigation' which directly contradicts the later commitment to mandatory arbitration. The 'Contradicted Text' section is the same as the changed text so the contradiction occurs between the two sentence in the same section. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
